presentation.name: Hi, I'm
presentation.title: Senior Software Engineer
presentation.subtitle: I build reliable and maintainable frontend systems. I have worked on projects at different stages, in multidisciplinary teams from diverse cultures and countries, from early-stage startups to companies with millions in ARR. My expertise includes design systems, web apps, and complete product lifecycles.
presentation.stack: React · Next.js · TypeScript · Playwright · Jest · Node.js · Express · MongoDB · PostgreSQL

hero.label: Engineering Principles
hero.heading: How I think about product and engineering decisions.
hero.body: Not just values I claim to have. These are insights I've come to after building projects at very different stages. Some I got right the first time, but most came with experience.

think.label: How I think
think.heading: The code is not the product. The solution is.
think.body: Messy code is not always a sign of bad decisions. Sometimes it was the right call at the time. Refactoring comes when you start working as people, not because the code is aesthetically displeasing, but when the gain is real.

decide.label: How I decide
decide.heading: Start it simple or write down why.
decide.body: The code outlasts the reasoning behind it. Without context, all that's left is speculation for those who come after. Documented tradeoffs are the only thing that closes that gap.

ship.label: How I ship
ship.heading: Optimize for the next person, not the next deploy.
ship.body: Fast delivery and clean systems aren't opposites. Naming, structure, and interfaces compound over time, for better or worse. Team speed is to write for the person who comes next.

collaborate.label: How I collaborate
collaborate.heading: To work as a team, not as an individual contributor.
collaborate.body: Unblocking someone beats shipping alone. Well-scoped PRs, readable code, clear decisions. None of it goes on a roadmap, but it's what keeps a team actually moving.

start.label: How I start
start.heading: Direction before momentum.
start.body: The first question is never which framework or stack to use. It's what the user actually needs to solve. With that clear, the architecture follows. Without that, you'll just arrive at the wrong place sooner.

terminal.description: Interactive terminal to explore my portfolio and get in touch.
terminal.tagline: Senior Software Engineer · React · Next.js · TypeScript

games.rps-description: The classic game of rock, paper, treasure, with real-time multiplayer options or against AI that learns from you.

whoami.line2: {years} years building reliable frontend systems across projects at different stages.
whoami.line3: From early-stage startups to multi-million dollar ARR companies. Design systems, web apps, and full product cycles.
whoami.line4: I'm experienced with multiple tools like React, Next.js, TypeScript, playwright and more.
